title: cell trace.deviations/trace.friction stay empty despite narrated deviations in outcome text/reports
detail: terminal-workspace-org-1 capped with trace.deviations:[] and trace.friction:null even though the cap outcome text and the worker report both explicitly describe 2 deviations (socket.rs, fake.rs). The structured fields meant to make deviations machine-discoverable for compounding/grooming passes weren't populated by the cap flow, so anyone querying trace.deviations programmatically misses real deviations that are only in prose.

title: bee-model-guard pins generation/extraction/review dispatches to read-only agent types that cannot execute cells
detail: Mid-session self-update introduced a hook requiring subagent_type bee-gather/bee-extract/bee-review for [bee-tier: ...] marker dispatches, but all 3 rendered .claude/agents/bee-*.md types are Read/Grep/Glob(+Bash for review)-only, no Edit/Write - they cannot implement+commit a cell as bee-swarming's own SKILL.md instructs. Blocks every execution-worker dispatch (full waves and AO14 single-worker tiny/small) until fixed upstream. Workaround in use: bare model param, no bee-tier marker, subagent_type general-purpose - works but is a workaround, not a fix.
